component.name: RufusArm64
component.summary: Create bootable USB drives on Ubuntu ARM64

description.p: RufusArm64 is an unofficial bootable-USB creator designed for Ubuntu on ARM64 computers.

It writes raw and ISOHybrid media, prepares compressed and common virtual-disk inputs, and creates Windows installation media using GPT or MBR, UEFI or x86-family BIOS/CSM, FAT32 or NTFS, WIM splitting, UEFI:NTFS, optional Windows PE drivers, and Secure Boot DBX checks.
Adds compressed and virtual-disk preparation, Microsoft DBX updates, PE/COFF revocation checks, and target-sized expansion limits.
Adds true x86/x86-64 Windows BIOS/CSM media, Windows PE driver auto-loading, source hashing, strict symlink-safe driver copies, and pinned legacy boot-code verification.
Adds manual FAT32/NTFS selection, verified Rufus UEFI:NTFS media, NTFS checks, and optional Windows driver-folder staging.
Adds GPT/MBR UEFI selection, FAT32 cluster-size controls, full formatting, a one-pass media check, and clearer From-image behavior.
Responsive graphical ARM64 release with Windows Setup options, compact WIM progress, direct partition detection, a bundled WIM engine, and expanded safety tests.
